compiled_state.title: test-batched.sh: 4 e2e tests fail in batch with 50s timeout but pass when run individually
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
During commit workflow test run using test-batched.sh --timeout=50, 4 tests were reported FAIL that pass when run individually:
- tests/scripts/test-bridge-observability-e2e.sh
- tests/scripts/test-bug-report-cli-validation.sh
- tests/scripts/test-bug-report-integration.sh
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-list-transition-comment-e2e.sh

## Expected Behavior
All tests should pass in the test-batched.sh batch run with the same 50s timeout they pass individually.

## Actual Behavior
The 4 tests show as FAIL in the batch summary. When run immediately afterward individually (bash <test-file>), they all pass within 30s. This suggests:
1. The tests exceed the 50s per-test timeout when run in sequence after many other tests (cumulative system load), OR
2. There is shared state or filesystem interference from earlier tests in the batch

## Root Cause Hypothesis
The most likely cause is that these e2e tests (which create git repos, ticket systems, and run long workflow sequences) accumulate enough state cleanup work from prior tests in the batch that their total wall-clock time exceeds 50s.

## Impact
The commit workflow Step 1 reports test failures that do not represent real failures, requiring the operator to re-run tests manually to confirm.

## Session
Observed during worktree-20260412-173753 session on 2026-04-12.
